objective: Clear the Friday return pile before cutoff
opposition: The inspected bargain model carries an undocumented scar and a retained image of a drowning boy
turn: Mira realizes the damage profile belongs to no listed renter and flags the file instead of closing it
consequence_cost: She loses throughput time and starts a pattern the audit software can notice

objective: Reconcile the anomaly with the renter record
opposition: The renter's assistant stonewalls and treats the body as disposable scandal control
turn: The assistant admits the client paid extra for memory dampening and still wants no statement
consequence_cost: Mira's curiosity becomes visible to management

objective: Process the body for disposal without attracting attention
opposition: Celia wants the hold shelf cleared and the system demands a final classification
turn: Mira places LM-127B on administrative hold and duplicates the residue scan
consequence_cost: She falsifies workflow and creates evidence of tampering

objective: Close a poor client's appeal before fees finalize
opposition: Policy says an undocumented injury voids coverage and turns repair into debt
turn: The client reveals she leases bargain bodies because her own wrists can no longer scrub hotel rooms
consequence_cost: Mira can no longer pretend the claims system is neutral

objective: Get the body repaired outside official channels
opposition: Tavi distrusts corporate employees and has neither time nor sterile supplies to waste
turn: He agrees to take the case if Mira brings cash, cases, and silence
consequence_cost: Mira enters an illegal partnership with someone who knows more than she does

objective: Secure Jo's back room as a repair site
opposition: Jo does not want cops, tissue leaks, or strangers dying behind her dryers
turn: Jo agrees if Mira and Tavi clean up after themselves and keep laundry customers insulated
consequence_cost: The clinic exists only on Jo's tolerance and neighborhood discretion

objective: Assemble a usable wetbench from salvaged parts
opposition: Old equipment fails and sterilants are scarce
turn: The first client arrives before the tools are stable
consequence_cost: The desk begins under pressure instead of preparation

objective: Repair a courier's shredded bargain knee quietly
opposition: The body keeps reacting to a subway-fall memory that belongs to no one present
turn: Mira sees the same kind of overlap signature she found in claims
consequence_cost: This is a pattern, not a single glitch

objective: Get through the first night without exposure
opposition: Word of mouth brings more clients than the room can safely hold
turn: Jo invents a laundry-membership cover story and the line stays
consequence_cost: The desk becomes a standing operation the neighborhood already expects

objective: Wipe and stabilize the held model before anyone checks the shelf
opposition: The body wakes mid-procedure in panic and addresses Mira directly
turn: LM-127B begs not to be returned and speaks from multiple incompatible memories
consequence_cost: Mira and Tavi can no longer treat the body as a defective object

objective: Decide whether to report, sedate, or hide the conscious model
opposition: Tavi wants caution, Mira knows reporting means pulp, and the body is listening
turn: The model names themself Lin from a borrowed stairwell memory
consequence_cost: Concealment becomes personal and criminal at once

objective: Get Lin safely through an ordinary morning
opposition: Lin keeps slipping between present objects and borrowed sensations
turn: Breakfast and borrowed clothes give Lin their first calm hour in the present
consequence_cost: Mira starts acting like a host instead of an investigator

objective: Introduce Lin to Gabe without a scene
opposition: Gabe assumes Mira has brought home a theft problem and Lin reads his defensiveness instantly
turn: Lin makes a shy joke about the apartment chairs and Gabe relaxes
consequence_cost: The household expands and so does the risk of exposure

objective: Decide whether Lin is temporary evidence or a resident
opposition: Mira fears attachment and Lin asks what happens to unwanted bodies
turn: Mira gives Lin a key and promises not to return them
consequence_cost: She binds herself emotionally and legally to Lin's survival

objective: Survive Celia's audit without losing access
opposition: Missing disposal paperwork and duplicate logs have already been flagged
turn: Celia schedules a deeper review instead of accepting a clerical explanation
consequence_cost: Mira's workday stops being a place of cover

objective: Minimize Yaret Sanz's suspicion
opposition: Sanz's questions are specific enough to show he already knows about anomalies
turn: Mira lies about filing lag and sees that Sanz does not believe her
consequence_cost: The investigation becomes personal rather than general

objective: Steal usable proof before the system locks her out
opposition: Office surveillance and short access windows leave almost no margin
turn: Mira copies the anomaly wafer from archived claims and walks out with it
consequence_cost: If caught, she faces tampering charges in addition to illegal repair work

objective: Repair a cleaner's sex-torn rental body fast enough to save her from fees
opposition: The injury is deep, the anesthetic patchy, and the body carries someone else's arousal panic
turn: Mira and Tavi fall into a rhythm of touch, instruction, and trust that feels intimate before either names it
consequence_cost: The repair succeeds but the working boundary between them thins dangerously

objective: Release the charge without destroying the partnership
opposition: Tavi's guilt and Mira's distrust could turn desire into recoil
turn: They have urgent sex in the laundry back room and afterward Tavi admits he wrote part of CommonFrame's aggregate-lattice shortcut
consequence_cost: Intimacy deepens even as Mira learns her lover helped make overlap profitable

objective: Move the growing client queue without panic or riot
opposition: The symptom mix is absurd, painful, and larger than Blue Hour can handle alone
turn: Lin starts doing intake and proves unnervingly good at calming people
consequence_cost: The clinic becomes dependent on Lin's social gift

objective: Keep a health inspector out of the back room
opposition: Public crowding and one unstable client make concealment brittle
turn: A client in a wedding body collapses into somebody else's honeymoon grief in full view
consequence_cost: The room tips from funny chaos into public exposure

objective: Contain the episode before officials see too much
opposition: The inspector is already suspicious and the clients are humiliated
turn: Lin reframes the breakdown as a grief-group scene and buys Jo time
consequence_cost: The staff save the day but with a performance too visible to stay harmless

objective: Decide whether to shrink operations after the scare
opposition: Demand keeps rising and clients have nowhere else to go
turn: Mira chooses to expand hours and keep taking cases
consequence_cost: Popularity becomes the engine of future danger

objective: Use the stolen wafer to locate the source of overlap
opposition: The data are incomplete and Tavi's old system knowledge is patchy under stress
turn: Tavi identifies Warehouse Nine as the disposal route for rich-residue bargain stock
consequence_cost: Mira now has a place, not just a theory

objective: Get inside Warehouse Nine and see what CommonFrame is hiding
opposition: Security drones, stench, and disposal crews make the warehouse hostile
turn: Mira and Lin discover pulped shells sorted by residual richness instead of defect type
consequence_cost: The company logic behind overlap becomes undeniable

objective: Leave with proof rather than only horror
opposition: An alarm begins and Lin is nearly transfixed by the bodies stacked like kin
turn: Mira steals a partial seed manifest but cannot access the full archive
consequence_cost: They gain proof that is damning yet incomplete and flee under risk

objective: Spend one ordinary day with Gabe without talking strategy
opposition: Debt calls, pain flares, and Mira's secrecy keep intruding
turn: Gabe admits he rents heavy-lift frames because his own spine cannot keep him employable
consequence_cost: Mira sees the system's survival function as clearly as its abuse

objective: Fit the new knowledge into a moral plan
opposition: Mira wants CommonFrame to be simply destroyable without collateral harm
turn: She realizes the poor will absorb the first damage if the body economy collapses overnight
consequence_cost: Her certainty about exposure as pure good breaks apart

objective: Bring Tavi into the full truth of Warehouse Nine
opposition: Tavi already understands enough to fear a reckless leak
turn: He argues for a surgical strategy that protects living overlaps and low-income users
consequence_cost: Mira loses the comfort of having an ally who wants exactly what she wants

objective: Get Lin to accept a quieter endgame
opposition: Lin refuses to remain hidden evidence and demands names, not just scandal control
turn: Lin declares that survival without recognition is another kind of return
consequence_cost: The conflict shifts from proof to whose future counts

objective: Handle Celia's private approach without being bought
opposition: Celia offers salary, housing, and legal insulation in exchange for silence
turn: Mira refuses and understands Celia is making the offer because she already knows the truth
consequence_cost: The alliance around Mira fractures while CommonFrame tests her price

objective: Keep a poor renter from being loaded with false blame
opposition: CommonFrame counsel frames overlap as illegal repair contamination and altered logs support them
turn: Mira watches the renter shrink under the language of liability
consequence_cost: If she stays quiet, the system wins in the exact way she now recognizes

objective: Choose between self-preservation and testimony
opposition: Speaking openly will cost Mira her wage, license, and any remaining cover
turn: Mira names overlap production under oath and is fired, fined, and escorted out
consequence_cost: She becomes economically exposed and publicly marked

objective: Keep Blue Hour operating after Mira's firing
opposition: Money is short and clients fear a post-hearing crackdown
turn: Jo rearranges laundry hours to conceal a busier clinic
consequence_cost: The clinic survives but is more obviously a collective than a side job

objective: Turn Lin's fragments into traceable identities
opposition: The fragments contradict one another and no formal database is safe
turn: Gabe recognizes a dock nickname that links several memories to one debtor pool
consequence_cost: The search becomes investigable rather than mystical

objective: Verify whether any donor was reused without meaningful consent
opposition: One family is suspicious and grief makes every question look predatory
turn: Mira learns at least one donor whose scan seeded overlap bodies is still alive and never knowingly signed for reuse
consequence_cost: The moral scandal expands beyond the dead and missing

objective: Decide what artifact can survive raids and disbelief
opposition: Digital files can be seized and everyone's memory is fragmenting under pressure
turn: The group starts a handwritten registry of overlaps, donors, scars, and known aliases
consequence_cost: The registry becomes their central weapon and responsibility

objective: Get clients out when Yaret's raid team arrives
opposition: Officers, inspectors, and half-repaired bodies clog the narrow space
turn: Jo buys seconds by shouting a gas leak and forcing confusion into the hallway
consequence_cost: Escape becomes possible but only barely

objective: Get Lin and the registry out alive
opposition: Gabe's damaged back, the officers' batons, and Lin's hesitation make flight messy
turn: Gabe takes a baton across his spine while shoving Lin into the steam chase with the ledger
consequence_cost: This is no longer a raid against equipment; it is bodily violence against the group

objective: Protect clients from state capture even if the clinic is lost
opposition: Tavi's system is still full of identities and procedures officers can seize
turn: He burns digital files as the wetbench catches and the room is gutted
consequence_cost: Blue Hour is destroyed and the group loses tools, money, and shelter

objective: Keep the group from collapsing into blame after the raid
opposition: Hospital pain, guilt, and exhaustion make every sentence dangerous
turn: Tavi finally names the exact nature of his complicity instead of hiding behind jargon
consequence_cost: Mira can hate him accurately now, which is different from walking away

objective: Choose whether Lin disappears or goes public
opposition: Testifying could expose Lin to law as property, but hiding leaves the system intact
turn: Lin decides to testify as a person and Mira commits to stand beside them
consequence_cost: Their strategy turns outward and becomes irreversible

objective: Prepare for tribunal with enough proof to survive procedure
opposition: Legal standards treat overlap as defect language, not person language
turn: Celia delivers the seed maps that prove deliberate donor aggregation
consequence_cost: The case gains the internal proof it previously lacked

objective: Get Lin and the registry admitted into the hearing
opposition: Yaret moves to exclude black-market evidence and witness standing
turn: Mira uses donor matches and the registry to force the tribunal to hear Lin
consequence_cost: The hearing opens wider than CommonFrame intended

objective: Make personhood undeniable in public
opposition: CommonFrame counsel insists composite memory is not selfhood
turn: Lin answers with embodied, contradictory, human specificity that the room cannot reduce to static
consequence_cost: Overlap bodies enter public reality for everyone listening

objective: Secure a material consequence beyond moral shock
opposition: The law still protects property categories more readily than persons
turn: The tribunal freezes CommonFrame production pending review but refuses immediate personhood recognition
consequence_cost: The win is partial and creates a dangerous vacuum

objective: Translate the tribunal win into safety for living overlaps
opposition: CommonFrame files bankruptcy and shuts maintenance access overnight
turn: People in bargain bodies flood the streets and Blue Hour with failing frames
consequence_cost: Victory mutates into a mass-care emergency

objective: Build an emergency triage site fast enough to matter
opposition: There are too many bodies, too few parts, and no legal coverage
turn: Jo seizes the old ferry terminal for a makeshift clinic and Celia arrives with archived maintenance maps
consequence_cost: A new community infrastructure forms out of collapse

objective: Choose between preserving the clean scandal narrative and doing immediate care
opposition: Every urgent case pulls labor away from media strategy and legal follow-up
turn: Mira chooses care first and lets the story get messier
consequence_cost: The book's ethics settle on maintenance over spectacle

objective: Make the cooperative feel like a place people can actually keep using
opposition: Exhaustion, patched systems, and unresolved law threaten to keep everything provisional
turn: The ferry terminal runs on schedules, food lines, and repair triage instead of panic
consequence_cost: Routine becomes a hard-won form of hope

objective: Imagine what counts as an ending when the system still exists
opposition: A new client arrives carrying an overlap scar from a different factory
turn: Mira understands the work is ongoing but no longer lonely
consequence_cost: The city now has a return address for the unreturnable
